Euclid Surface Photometry Pipeline

Euclid Surface Photometry Pipeline


Version: 0.1.2 | Paper: Sánchez-Alarcón et al. (2026, A&A) — Euclid Quick Data Release (Q1): Disc breaks highlight the structural evolution of galaxies through cosmic time

Overview

This package implements an automatic surface brightness profile analysis pipeline designed to extract and characterise the radial light distribution of disc galaxies observed by the Euclid space mission. The pipeline was applied to 8 748 disc galaxies selected from the Euclid Quick Release 1 (Q1) dataset, covering 63.1 deg² across the three Euclid Deep Fields (EDF-N, EDF-S, EDF-Fornax).

The pipeline produces, for each galaxy:

  • Multi-band image cutouts centred on the source and combined masks that remove contaminating neighbours while preserving the low surface brightness outskirts of the target (Module 1).

  • Radial position-angle (PA) and ellipticity (ε) profiles measured via elliptical isophote fitting (Module 2).

  • Per-band surface brightness profiles calibrated to a common sky background measured adaptively at the edge of each galaxy (Module 3).

  • Integrated photometric and structural parameters — asymptotic magnitudes, effective radii, isophotal radii, concentration indices, and more (Module 4).

  • Automatic disc break classification into Type I (pure exponential), Type II (down-bending), Type III (up-bending), and composite (e.g. Type II+III) profiles, together with best-fit structural parameters for each disc component (Module 5).

The pipeline is parallelised with Python’s multiprocessing.Pool and is driven by a YAML configuration file; see Usage for details.
